rationale: 50 consecutive Event ID 4625 (failed logon) events were generated within approximately 181 milliseconds (A0 through EV-049), all originating from the same process (C:\Tools\[x]\[x].exe) under subject user-01, targeting 50 distinct usernames that are random-looking 8-character uppercase strings inconsistent with real account names — a pattern strongly indicative of automated local account enumeration or credential stuffing via the LogonUser API (Advapi, logon type 2). Sub-status 0xc0000064 confirms each target username does not exist, suggesting the tool is iterating through a generated username list rather than targeting known accounts. The non-standard tool path (C:\Tools\) and the machine-generated username pattern rule out benign explanations; no successful logon or process creation context is present to confirm further compromise, but the activity itself constitutes confirmed malicious reconnaissance/brute-force behavior.
